---
title: "Final Project"
format: 
  html:
    toc: true
    embed-resources: true
    code-fold: true
    code-line-numbers: true
    code-summary: "SHOW ME THE CODE!"
    code-tools: true
editor: visual
---

```{r}
library(haven)        
library(janitor)     
library(dplyr)      
library(tidyr)        
library(ggplot2)      
library(lme4)         
library(emmeans)      
library(performance)  
library(broom.mixed)  
library(WeMix)
library(splines)
library(tibble)

# 1) Read & clean -------------------------------------------------------
nlsy_path <- Sys.getenv("NLSY_DTA", unset = "./data/NLSY.dta")
model_cache_dir <- Sys.getenv("MODEL_CACHE_DIR", unset = ".")

df <- read_dta(nlsy_path) %>%
  clean_names() %>%
  mutate(
    race     = as_factor(race),
    sex      = as_factor(sex),
    self_emp = as_factor(self_emp),
    nlsy79   = as_factor(nlsy79),
    nlsy97   = as_factor(nlsy97)
  )

# 2) Missing‐data summary -----------------------------
missing_report <- df %>%
  summarise(across(everything(), ~ sum(is.na(.)))) %>%
  pivot_longer(everything(), names_to="variable", values_to="n_missing") %>%
  mutate(pct_missing=100*n_missing/nrow(df)) %>%
  arrange(desc(pct_missing))
print(missing_report)

# 3) Imputation & filtering -----------------------------------
df2 <- df %>%
  group_by(id) %>%
  arrange(year) %>%
  # interpolate age if ≥2 observations
  mutate(
    age_interp = if(sum(!is.na(age))>=2)
                   approx(year[!is.na(age)], age[!is.na(age)],
                          xout=year, rule=2)$y
                 else NA_real_,
    age        = coalesce(age, age_interp)
  ) %>%
  fill(afqt, .direction="downup") %>%
  mutate(
    hgc_max = if(all(is.na(hgc))) NA_real_ else max(hgc, na.rm=TRUE),
    hgc     = coalesce(hgc, hgc_max)
  ) %>%
  fill(self_emp, .direction="downup") %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  filter(!is.na(age), !is.na(afqt), !is.na(hgc), !is.na(self_emp)) %>%
  select(-age_interp, -hgc_max)

# 4) Derive model vars & scaling ---------------------
df_model <- df2 %>%
  mutate(
    age_c      = age  - mean(age, na.rm=TRUE),
    afqt_c     = afqt - mean(afqt, na.rm=TRUE),
    hgc_c      = hgc  - mean(hgc, na.rm=TRUE),
    hours_100  = hourswork/100,
    afqt_s     = as.numeric(scale(afqt_c)),
    hgc_s      = as.numeric(scale(hgc_c)),
    log_income = log1p(income),
    cohort     = case_when(nlsy79=="1"~"NLSY79",
                           nlsy97=="1"~"NLSY97") %>% factor(),
    w_scaled   = weight/mean(weight, na.rm=TRUE),
    id         = factor(id)
  )
df_model <- df_model %>%
  mutate(wgt_id = 1)
```

```{r}
# Check how many participants dropped after cleaning
# Before cleaning
n_obs_init <- nrow(df)
n_ids_init <- dplyr::n_distinct(df$id)

# After cleaning
n_obs_final <- nrow(df_model)
n_ids_final <- dplyr::n_distinct(df_model$id)

dropped_ids <- n_ids_init - n_ids_final

cat(
  "Initial sample: ",  n_obs_init,  " person‐year obs, ",
                       n_ids_init, " unique IDs\n",
  "Final   sample: ",  n_obs_final, " person‐year obs, ",
                       n_ids_final, " unique IDs\n",
  "Participants dropped: ", dropped_ids, "\n"
)

```

```{r}
# EDA

library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(scales)

# Palettes
pal_col  <- c("Salaried" = "#F8766D", "Self Emp" = "#00BFC4")
pal_fill <- pal_col

# Overall density of raw income by employment
ggplot(df_model, aes(x = income, fill = self_emp)) +
  geom_density(alpha = .4) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = pal_fill) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    labels = dollar_format(),
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Density of Raw Income by Employment",
    x     = "Income ($)",
    fill  = "Employment"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

# Density of positive incomes by employment
ggplot(filter(df_model, income > 0),
       aes(x = income, fill = self_emp)) +
  geom_density(alpha = .4) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = pal_fill) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = dollar_format()) +
  
  labs(
    title = "Density of Income (>0) by Employment",
    x     = "Income ($)",
    fill  = "Employment"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

# Age Distribution by Employment Type

ggplot(df_model, aes(x = age, fill = self_emp)) +
  geom_density(alpha = .4, size = 0.2) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    name   = "Employment Type",
    values = c("Salaried" = "#F8766D", "Self Emp" = "#00BFC4")
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Age Distribution by Employment Type",
    x     = "Age",
    y     = "Density of Individuals"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14)

# Spaghetti: Raw income trajectories for 100 IDs
set.seed(1)
sam_ids <- sample(unique(df_model$id), 100)
ggplot(filter(df_model, id %in% sam_ids),
       aes(x = age, y = income, group = id, color = self_emp)) +
  geom_line(alpha = .6) +
  scale_color_manual(values = pal_col) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = dollar_format()) +
  labs(
    title = "Spaghetti: Income vs Age (100 Random IDs)",
    x     = "Age",
    y     = "Income ($)",
    color = "Employment"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

# Mean raw‐income over age (conditional on income > 0)
df_pos <- filter(df_model, income > 0)
ggplot(df_pos, aes(age, income, color = self_emp, fill = self_emp)) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean,    geom = "line",  size = 1.1) +
  stat_summary(fun.data = mean_cl_boot, geom = "ribbon", alpha = .3) +
  scale_color_manual(values = pal_col) +
  scale_fill_manual(values  = pal_fill) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = dollar_format()) +
  labs(
    title = "Mean Income over Age (Conditional on Income > 0)",
    x     = "Age",
    y     = "Mean Income ($)",
    color = "Employment",
    fill  = "Employment"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()


# Distribution of annual hours worked by employment
ggplot(df_model, aes(x = hours_100 * 100, fill = self_emp)) +
  geom_histogram(position = "identity", alpha = .5, bins = 30) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = pal_fill) +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of Annual Hours Worked by Employment Type",
    x     = "Hours Worked",
    y     = "Count",
    fill  = "Employment"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

# Hours‐worked box plot by employment type

ggplot(df_model, aes(x = self_emp, y = hourswork, fill = self_emp)) +
  geom_violin(alpha = 0.3, color = NA) +
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.1, outlier.size = 0.5, color = "black") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = pal_col) +
  labs(
    title = "Annual Hours Worked by Employment Type",
    x     = "Employment Type",
    y     = "Hours Worked (per year)",
    fill  = "Employment"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()


# Smoothed Income Trajectories by Gender & Race
ggplot(
  df_model %>% filter(income > 0),
  aes(x = age, y = income, color = self_emp, fill = self_emp)
) +
  geom_smooth(
    method  = "loess",
    formula = y ~ x,
    span    = 0.3,
    se      = TRUE,
    size    = 1.2,
    alpha   = 0.3
  ) +
  facet_grid(sex ~ race) +
  scale_color_manual(name = "Employment", values = pal_col) +
  scale_fill_manual(name = "Employment", values = pal_fill) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    labels = comma_format(prefix = "$"),    # or use dollar_format()
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, .05))
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Smoothed Income Trajectories by Gender & Race",
    x     = "Age",
    y     = "Income"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

```

```{r}
# 6) WeMix model sequence & comparisons -------------------------------

#Tuning defaults
mix_args <- list(max_iteration = 1000,  
                 nQuad         = 13,    
                 verbose       = FALSE)

# 6.1) Model 1: intercept + age
#wm1 <- do.call(mix, c(
#  list(formula = log_income ~ age_c + (1 | id),
#       data    = df_model,
#       weights = c("w_scaled","wgt_id")),
#  mix_args
#))

#saveRDS(wm1, file = "wm1.rds")

wm1 <- readRDS(file.path(model_cache_dir, "wm1.rds"))


# 6.2) Model 2: + main effect of self_emp
#wm2 <- do.call(mix, c(
#  list(formula = log_income ~ age_c + self_emp + (1 | id),
#       data    = df_model,
#       weights = c("w_scaled","wgt_id")),
#  mix_args
#))

#saveRDS(wm2, file = "wm2.rds")

wm2 <- readRDS(file.path(model_cache_dir, "wm2.rds"))

# 6.3) Model 3: + age × self_emp interaction
#wm3 <- do.call(mix, c(
#  list(formula = log_income ~ age_c * self_emp + (1 | id),
#       data    = df_model,
#       weights = c("w_scaled","wgt_id")),
#  mix_args
#))

#saveRDS(wm3, file = "wm3.rds")

wm3 <- readRDS(file.path(model_cache_dir, "wm3.rds"))

# 6.4) Model 4: add AFQT, HGC, hours + random slope on age
#wm4 <- do.call(mix, c(
#  list(formula = log_income ~ age_c * self_emp
#                         + afqt_s + hgc_s + hours_100
#                         + (1 + age_c | id),
#       data    = df_model,
#       weights = c("w_scaled","wgt_id")),
#  mix_args
#))

#saveRDS(wm4, file = "wm4.rds")

wm4 <- readRDS(file.path(model_cache_dir, "wm4.rds"))

# 6.5) Model 5: add race, sex, cohort
#wm5 <- do.call(mix, c(
#  list(formula = log_income ~ age_c * self_emp
#                         + afqt_s + hgc_s + hours_100
#                         + race + sex + cohort
#                         + (1 + age_c | id),
#       data    = df_model,
#       weights = c("w_scaled","wgt_id")),
#  mix_args
#))

#saveRDS(wm5, file = "wm5.rds")

wm5 <- readRDS(file.path(model_cache_dir, "wm5.rds"))


```

```{r}
# Model metric comparison for wm1…wm5 

library(purrr) 
library(knitr)

# 1) Read in your five models
models <- list(
  wm1,
  wm2,
  wm3,
  wm4,
  wm5
)

# 2) Number of observations (for BIC)
n_obs <- nrow(df_model)

# 3) Build a metrics table, including Model names
model_metrics <- enframe(models, name = "Model", value = "fit") %>%
  mutate(
    logLik = map_dbl(fit, ~ .x$lnl),
    npar   = map_int(fit, ~ length(.x$coef))
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    AIC = -2 * logLik + 2 * npar,
    BIC = -2 * logLik + log(n_obs) * npar
  ) %>%
  arrange(AIC) %>%
  mutate(
    deltaAIC = AIC - min(AIC),
    weight   = exp(-0.5 * deltaAIC) / sum(exp(-0.5 * deltaAIC))
  ) %>%
  select(Model, logLik, npar, AIC, BIC, deltaAIC, weight)

# 4) Render with knitr::kable
kable(
  model_metrics,
  digits  = c(0, 1, 0, 1, 1, 1, 3),
  caption = "Model comparison: wm1–wm5"
)
```

```{r model-comparison-table, echo=FALSE, warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}


library(dplyr)
library(purrr)
library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)


# 2) Number of observations (for BIC)
n_obs <- nrow(df_model)

# 3) Build metrics table
model_metrics <- tibble(
  Model      = names(models),
  `Log-Lik`  = map_dbl(models, ~ .x$lnl),
  Parameters = map_int(models, ~ length(.x$coef))
) %>%
  mutate(
    AIC         = -2 * `Log-Lik` + 2 * Parameters,
    BIC         = -2 * `Log-Lik` + log(n_obs) * Parameters
  ) %>%
  arrange(AIC) %>%
  mutate(
    `ΔAIC`      = AIC - min(AIC),
    `Akaike Wt` = exp(-0.5 * `ΔAIC`) / sum(exp(-0.5 * `ΔAIC`))
  ) %>%
  # round in-place
  mutate(
    `Log-Lik`  = round(`Log-Lik`, 1),
    AIC         = round(AIC,       1),
    BIC         = round(BIC,       1),
    `ΔAIC`      = round(`ΔAIC`,    1),
    `Akaike Wt` = signif(`Akaike Wt`, 3)
  )

# 4) Print with knitr::kable
knitr::kable(
  model_metrics,
  format    = "html",
  caption   = "Table X. Comparison of WeMix Models (wm1–wm5)",
  digits    = c(0, 1, 0, 1, 1, 1, 3),
  align     = c("l", rep("r", 6)),
  booktabs  = TRUE
)
```

```{r}
# Summarize 
summary(wm5)


```

```{r}
model_wm <- wm5

```

```{r}
performance::r2(wm5)
```

```{r manual-predictions-wm5, warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}

library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(lme4)   # for nobars()

# 1) Sample 25 IDs & drop other levels
set.seed(1)
ids25 <- sample(unique(df_model$id), 25)

df_pred <- df_model %>%
  filter(id %in% ids25) %>%
  mutate(id = droplevels(id))  # now only 25 levels remain

# 2) Extract fixed‐effect coefficients
b <- wm5$coef
# 3) Build the fixed‐effects design matrix
#    strip off the random‐part from the formula
fixef_form <- lme4::nobars(wm5$call$formula)
Xb <- model.matrix(fixef_form, data = df_pred)

# 4) Compute the fixed‐part of the prediction
fixed_pred <- as.vector(Xb %*% b)

# 5) Extract the id‐level random effects matrix
uMat <- wm5$ranefMat[["id"]]  
#    (rows = id levels, cols = "(Intercept)" and "age_c")

# 6) Compute the random‐part for each row
random_pred <- 
  uMat[ as.character(df_pred$id), "(Intercept)" ] + 
  uMat[ as.character(df_pred$id), "age_c"      ] * df_pred$age_c

# 7) Total linear predictor on the link scale
df_pred <- df_pred %>%
  mutate(
    pred_log_manual = fixed_pred + random_pred
  )

# 8) Plot actual vs. manual‐predicted
ggplot(df_pred, aes(x = age, group = id)) +
  geom_line(aes(y = log_income), color = "grey70") +
  geom_line(aes(y = pred_log_manual),
            color = "#00BFC4", linetype = "dashed") +
  facet_wrap(~ id, scales = "free_y", ncol = 5) +
  labs(
    title = "Actual vs. Manual‐Predicted Log-Income (25 IDs)",
    x     = "Age", 
    y     = "Log(Income)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 10) +
  theme(strip.text = element_text(size = 8))



```

```{r}
df_pred %>% 
  summarise(
    RMSE = sqrt(mean((log_income - pred_log_manual)^2)),
    R2   = cor(log_income, pred_log_manual)^2
  )

```
